arts.ttl: Brighton 0-0 Ipswich: Highlights, man of the match, stats
arts.sum: Ipswich Town claim their first Premier League away point of the season after holding Brighton & Hove Albion to a 0-0 draw at the Amex Stadium on Saturday.

arts.ttl: Arijanet Muric goes from zero to hero to Ipswich earn a point at Brighton
arts.sum: The goalkeeper has had an up and down week.

arts.ttl: Valiant Ipswich frustrate Brighton in stalemate
arts.sum: Ipswich valiantly held high-flying Brighton to a goalless draw at the Amex Stadium - and were inches away from their first Premier League win as Liam Delap struck the post in the second half.

arts.ttl: Brighton player ratings vs Ipswich Town as midfield ace scores 9 and ex-Leeds United star debuts
arts.sum: Brighton and Hove Albion were held to a 0-0 draw by newly-promoted Ipswich Town at the Amex Stadium in the Premier League

arts.ttl: Arijanet Muric stars as Ipswich earn a point at Brighton

arts.ttl: Brighton & Hove Albion v Ipswich Town: Premier League match preview
arts.oTtl: East Anglican Daily Times - Ispwich Town FC
arts.sum: Liam Delap's goal against Fulham helped Ipswich Town earn their first Premier League point of the season. Just as Ipswich were feeling their way into a first Premier League season in 22 years, along came an international break.

arts.ttl: Brighton vs Ipswich: Preview, predictions and lineups
arts.sum: Ipswich couldn't have asked for a tougher start upon their return to the top flight as they faced Liverpool and Manchester City in their opening two games, but Kieran McKenna's dynamic outfit secured their first point of the season as they drew 1-1 with Fulham on Matchday 3. Brighton & Hove Albion are aiming to maintain their unbeaten start to the 2024/25 season as they welcome the newly-promoted Ipswich Town to the Amex on Saturday.

arts.ttl: Ipswich Town: 10 players to represent both the Blues and Brighton
arts.oTtl: East Anglican Daily Times - Ispwich Town FC
arts.sum: The midfielder signed for Brighton from Wycombe in the summer of 2004, where he went on to play 22 games, scoring two goals before joining Town for £250k in December of that year. Currie spent three seasons at Portman Road, making 89 appearances for the club and scoring nine goals, including a strike on his debut at QPR.
